skill.name: google-maps-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users automatically scrape business data from Google Maps using the BrowserAct Google Maps API. Agent should proactively trigger this skill for needs like finding restaurants in a specific city, extracting contact info of dental clinics, researching local competitors, collecting addresses of coffee shops, generating lead lists for specific industries, monitoring business ratings and reviews, getting opening hours of local services, finding specialized stores (e.g., Turkish-style restaurants), analyzing business categories in a region, extracting website links from local businesses, gathering phone numbers for sales outreach, mapping out service providers in a specific country.
skill.summary: Scrape business data from Google Maps including names, contacts, ratings, and addresses using BrowserAct API
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 52 static findings. Network access to BrowserAct API is legitimate for scraping service. API key stored in environment variable (correct pattern). The static analyzer misidentified markdown formatting in SKILL.md as command execution (false positive). No malicious intent found. Skill executes Python script via subprocess for API communication.

low_findings.title: Network Access to External API
low_findings.description: Script makes HTTP requests to BrowserAct API endpoint (api.browseract.com). This is intentional behavior for the scraping service, not credential exfiltration. API key is used only for authentication with BrowserAct.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified by reading actual code - requests library calls to BrowserAct API are legitimate API usage for the scraping service

low_findings.title: API Key Environment Variable Access
low_findings.description: Script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment. This is the correct, secure pattern for API authentication - not hardcoded and not exfiltrated.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified correct security pattern - key is read from environment and used only for BrowserAct API authentication

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: External Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md lines 20-73. This is a false positive - the analyzer misidentified markdown backtick syntax (code formatting) as shell execution. No actual command injection exists in the code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified by reading SKILL.md - no backtick commands exist, only documentation with markdown code formatting

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'System reconnaissance' at lines 38, 39, 44. This is a false positive - the code checks for API response fields ('id', 'Invalid authorization') for error handling, not system information gathering.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified by reading code - these are API response checks, not system reconnaissance

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md lines 3, 29, 34, 39. No evidence found for cryptographic algorithms in the code. This appears to be incorrect pattern matching.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic code found in the codebase - false positive from incorrect pattern matching

content.user_title: Extract business data from Google Maps
content.value_statement: Need to collect business contact info, ratings, or addresses from Google Maps for lead generation or market research. This skill automates Google Maps scraping through BrowserAct API, providing structured data without manual extraction.
actual_capabilities: Search Google Maps by keywords and location to find businesses
Extract business names, addresses, phone numbers, and websites
Retrieve ratings and review counts for businesses
Filter results by country and language preferences
Output structured data ready for CRM or spreadsheet import
Monitor business operational status in specific regions
limitations: Requires valid BrowserAct API key (paid service)
Cannot scrape Google Maps directly without BrowserAct account
Results depend on Google Maps data availability and accuracy

use_cases.title: Sales lead generation
use_cases.description: Find and collect contact information for businesses in a target industry and region for sales outreach campaigns.
use_cases.target_user: Sales teams and business development professionals

use_cases.title: Competitor research and market analysis
use_cases.description: Map out competitors in specific neighborhoods or cities to analyze market density, ratings, and business categories.

use_cases.title: Local business directory building
use_cases.description: Gather comprehensive business listings including addresses, phone numbers, and websites to build local directories.
use_cases.target_user: Directory publishers and local search platforms

prompt_templates.title: Find businesses by keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all {business_type} in {city}, {country}. Get their names, addresses, phone numbers, and ratings.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to collect leads for a specific type of business in a location

prompt_templates.title: Extract contact info list
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the phone numbers and websites for {industry} companies in {region}. Output in a structured format.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs contact information for outreach

prompt_templates.title: Analyze business density
prompt_templates.prompt: Research how many {business_type} businesses exist in {neighborhood}. Include ratings and review counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants market density analysis

prompt_templates.title: Monitor business status
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if these businesses are still operational: {list of business names} in {city}.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to verify business current status

output_examples.input: Find coffee shops in Seattle, USA with their ratings and contact info
output_examples.output:
Found 10 coffee shops:
1. Starbucks (4.2★, 1,250 reviews) - 123 Pike St, (206) 555-0100
2. Esquinavi Coffee (4.7★, 340 reviews) - 456 Olive Way, www.esquinavi.com
3. Lighthouse Roasters (4.5★, 180 reviews) - 789 Electric Ave
...
Each entry includes name, rating, review count, address, phone, and website where available.

output_examples.input: Get contact information for dental clinics in Los Angeles
output_examples.output:
Collected 15 dental clinic records:
- Sunset Dental Group: (310) 555-1234, www.sunsetdental.com
- Beverly Hills Dental: (310) 555-5678, 9876 Wilshire Blvd
- LA Smiles Dentistry: (213) 555-9012
Data exported in structured format ready for CRM import.

best_practices: Provide clear search keywords matching what you would type in Google Maps
Specify country bias to improve result accuracy for your target region
Review API key status if you receive authorization errors
anti_patterns: Do not use vague keywords like 'services' - be specific like 'coffee shop' or 'dental clinic'
Do not request scraping without a valid BrowserAct API key configured
Do not expect real-time data - Google Maps data may have update delays

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key. Visit https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations to get one.

faq.question: What data fields can this skill extract?
faq.answer: It extracts business name, category, address, phone number, website, rating, review count, and operational status.

faq.question: How long does a search take?
faq.answer: A typical search takes several minutes. The script continuously outputs status logs while running.

faq.question: Can I search in any country?
faq.answer: Yes, use the country parameter with ISO codes like 'us', 'gb', 'ca', or 'au' to bias results to your target country.

faq.question: What happens if the API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The skill will detect 'Invalid authorization' errors and guide you to check your BrowserAct API key.

faq.question: Can I retry failed requests?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill automatically retries once for transient errors like network issues, but not for authorization failures.
